input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate its configuration to the modern `.runsettings` format. Let me look at their files to understand the current setup.

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** by modern MSTest v3. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent for your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- 30-second per-test timeout (was TestTimeout property in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Deployment is automatic in modern MSTest; set to false if not needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file which is the legacy format. Modern MSTest (v3+) uses `.runsettings` instead. I need to help them migrate. Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file and explain the changes.

The `.testsettings` file has:
1. Assembly resolution with `useLoadContext="true"` — not needed in modern MSTest
2. `TestTimeout` of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. Code coverage data collector

The equivalent `.runsettings` would configure:
- `TestTimeout` → `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` under `<MSTest>`


- Code coverage → `<DataCollector>` under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>`
- Deployment is handled differently in modern MSTest

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- 30-second per-test timeout (was TestTimeout property in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Deployment is automatic in modern MSTest; set to false if not needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
I've created `local.runsettings` as the replacement. Here's what changed:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` |
| `AssemblyResolution / useLoadContext` | No longer needed — modern MSTest handles this automatically |
| Code Coverage `DataCollector` (v1 URI) | Updated to v2 URI under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

**To use it**, either:
- Run: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- Or add `<RunSettingsFilePath>local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>` to a `<PropertyGroup>` in your `.csproj`

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` after verifying the new settings work.
